English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
DeForest Douglas Diver railroad photographs
Diver, DeForest Douglas, 1878-1958
1948
3.4 cubic feet. 3.4 cubic feet.
ca. 1870-1950. Original photographs, glass plates and other negatives, glossy prints. Original photographs, glass plates and other negatives, and glossy prints, mainly of the New York, Ontario, and Western Railway, taken by Mr. Diver and others in Delaware, Madison, Oneida, Oswego, Otsego, and Sullivan Counties, New York. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 8 ======= 8 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2081732
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
P. Pelley
Date completed:
October 1984
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, January 2016
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
A collection of DeForest Douglas Diver, 1878-1958, railroad engineer and photographer, consisting of original photographs, glass plates and other negatives, and glossy prints, mainly of the New York, Ontario, and Western Railway. Subjects include employee groups, locomotives, passenger and freight cars, stations at Middletown and elsewhere, roundhouse and yards at Middleton, flooded and snowbound tracks, construction work and heavy equipment, the Hawk Mountain tunnel, and various wrecks. Photographs were taken by Mr. Diver and others at Cadosia, Cooks Falls, Fishs Eddy, Sidney, Delaware County; Morrisville, Oneida, Randallsville, Solsville, Madison County; Clinton, Oneida County; Constantia, Fulton, Oswego County; South New Berlin, Otsego County; Callicoon, Ferndale, Hankins, Hurleyville, Liberty, Summitville, Sullivan County.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Photograph files
Boxes 1-6
Series II. Negatives only
Box7
Supplemental Lists
List of N.Y.O. & W. engines usually with a coal car in the Diver RR Collection, glossy print and glass plate of each; compiled by N. Dean, 4-6-66. In Box [ ] unless noted otherwise.Engine #
1
5
11
26 [28]
30
31
41
49
51
74
75
78
103
105
139
143
225
227
247
248
303
304
352
358
360
362
401
405
453
Franklin
and a few unnumbered engines.
List of N.Y.O. & W. vestibule chair (passenger) cars in the Diver RR Collection, glossy print and glass plate of each; compiled by N. Dean, 4-6-66.Vestibule chair cars #
85
86
List of boxcar photographs in the Diver RR Collection; compiled by N. Dean, 2/25/64
One boxcar in background; # impossible to read.
glass plate
Long line of boxcars at station; # impossible to read; incl. Lehigh Valley car; neg. # 40.
glass plate
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 2211.
glass plate
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 2211 (second view).
glass plate
N.Y.O. & W. engine 360 and boxcar (?) 15432; neg. #E17.
glass plate & print
Boxcar 2212.
glass plate
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 2816 with engine 99.
print
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 2327.
print
Train on track; engine, boxcar, and 2 passenger cars; no numbers [797 on back of picture].
print
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 9257 at great distance.
picture postcard
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 9623 with engine 362; neg. #E16.
glass plate
N.Y.O. & W. boxcar 9584 [incorrectly captioned as "91 - Buell Brothers' Taxicab, Norwich, N.Y."].
print
Additional information on contents of Diver RR Photograph Collection
Negative and print of picture of inspection locomotive #28 made, June 26, 1963; print sent of Lucius Beebe (see general corres. file) and negative filed in collection.
Collection does NOT include a picture of inspection locomotive #28 (see letter from Leonard Rice, 9/7/63).
For checklist of boxcar photos made 2/64, see attached list.
For checklist of N.Y.O. & W. engine and vestibule chair car photos made 4/66, see attached list.
Additional Description
Report of the Curator and Archivist, 1958-62
Available Copies:
Photographs from the collection have been digitized and are accessible through Cornell Library's Flickr site: http://www.flickr.com/photos/cornelluniversitylibrary/collections/72157621733952250/ .
Photographs from the collection are also accessible through the RMC image portal..
Cite As:
DeForest Douglas Diver Railroad Photographs, #1948. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Railroad engineer, photographer.
Photographs. Walton (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Sullivan County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Otsego County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Oswego County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Oneida County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Madison County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Delaware County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Families -- New York (State) -- Walton. Railroads -- Pictorial works. Railroads -- New York (State) Railroad stations. Railroads -- Design and construction. Railroad accidents. Hawk Mountain Tunnel. New York, Ontario, and Western Railway Co

Wilson on 12/26/1897)[Glass neg. only]6no numberNegativesSanta Fe type Class X, Middletown water column in foregroundEng. 352, 2-10-2glass7Order card for NYO&W Class Y mountain type engineEng. 401-410, 4-8-2glass7Pop Weehawker, DeForest (Pat) Diver, 1glass7Canal towpath, Summitville, N.Y., seventeen mile level at Summitville, N.Y.glass7Minnewaska Road (3 glass plates)glass7Unidentified (2 glass plates)glass7Two Negatives7Camelback Dickson, Sam St. John, Bob Oles "Dichen" at Northfield TunnelEng. 151, 2-8-0film7Harvey and Lozier on inspectionEng. #26film7Possibly Sidney Stationfilm7Mayfield Yard Shop buildings, Mayfield, Pa.film7Box cars, furniture & auto cars [wreck] (2 negs & 2 prints)9200-9751film7Camelback Mogul with baggage, RPO, & coach on trestleEng. 246, 2-6-0film7Trestle (2 negs.)film7Five views, type Sante Fe, brand new 1916 (5 negs.)Eng. 351, 2-10-2film7Shop crew, locomotive shop, Middletown, N.Y. (8 negs.)film7Cadosia, N.Y. yards, coal storage, trestle (3 negs.)film7Mogul type CamelbackEng. 274, 2-6-0film7Wreck at Borden's condensed milk plant (2 negs.)film7Switching milk cars on New Berlin Branch?Engine 217film7Northfield Tunnel portalfilm7Scenes along the right-of-way and the D&H Canal (4 negs.)film7Steam derrick #1film7Three Negatives7Scene on Scranton Division near Orson, Pa. cut where heavy drifts of snow accumulated to be plowed outfilm7Orson, Pa. double tracked with siding going to ice housefilm7Orson, Pa. looking northeast toward creamery and ice housefilm7Steam derrick #2 and trailer flat (2 negs.)film7Coaling tower at Mayfield Yard, Mayfield, PA. and ash pitfilm7Cadosia Station and tracks, Cadosia, N.Y.film7Unidentified (32 negs.)film7 ======= PhotographsClass X #55277(no gl. negative)11
